public_title: Phase II study of immunotherapy (pembrolizumab) in recurrent or metastatic head and neck squamous cell carcinoma following platinum failure in Indian patients
scientific_title: Single-arm phase II trial evaluating pembrolizumab monotherapy in platinum-refractory recurrent or metastatic HNSCC: PD-L1 expression and outcomes in an Indian cohort
health_condition: Recurrent or metastatic head and neck squamous cell carcinoma (HNSCC)

intervention.name: Pembrolizumab 200 mg IV every 3 weeks
intervention.details: Anti-PD-1 monoclonal antibody, administered until disease progression or unacceptable toxicity, maximum 35 cycles

comparator.name: None (single arm)
comparator.details: No comparator; response rate compared to historical controls

inclusion_criteria: Histologically confirmed recurrent or metastatic HNSCC of oral cavity, oropharynx, hypopharynx, or larynx; Disease progression on or after platinum-based chemotherapy; Age 18 years or older; ECOG performance status 0, 1, or 2; Measurable disease per RECIST v1.1; Adequate organ function; PD-L1 expression status assessed by IHC (any CPS score eligible); No active autoimmune disease requiring systemic treatment within past 2 years; Signed informed consent
exclusion_criteria: Prior treatment with anti-PD-1, anti-PD-L1, or anti-CTLA-4 therapy; Active autoimmune disease; Systemic corticosteroids (>10 mg/day prednisone equivalent) within 7 days of first dose; Active infection requiring systemic therapy; HIV positive with CD4 <350 cells/μL; Pregnancy or unwillingness to use contraception; Nasopharyngeal carcinoma histology
primary_sponsor: Department of Biotechnology, Government of India
